sections.title: Test Coverage Analysis — Distill (book_shorts)
sections.content: **Baseline: there is no automated test suite.** `npm test` only runs `scripts/validate-content.mjs`, a content linter — it checks JSON/Markdown shape, not application logic. No test framework (`vitest`/`jest`), no `@testing-library/react`, no `jsdom` config exist. Every function below has **zero** coverage today.

sections.title: Recommended setup (prerequisite)
sections.content:
```bash
npm i -D vitest jsdom @testing-library/react @testing-library/jest-dom @testing-library/user-event
```

```ts
// vite.config.ts — add
test: {
  environment: 'jsdom',
  setupFiles: ['./src/test/setup.ts'],
}
```

```ts
// src/test/setup.ts
import '@testing-library/jest-dom/vitest'
```

---

sections.title: 1. `src/lib/content.ts` — highest ROI, pure logic
2. `src/lib/markdown.tsx`
3. `src/lib/prefs.ts`
4. `src/lib/progress.ts`
5. `src/lib/store.ts`
6. `src/lib/tts.ts` — largest gap, most complex logic
7. Components — `AudioPlayer`, `NavBar`, `Cover`
8. Pages — integration-level gaps (the biggest blind spot)
9. `scripts/validate-content.mjs` (the current "test" script)
sections.content:
This is a build-time linter with zero tests of its own, despite being wired to `npm test`.

- **Duplication risk:** it reimplements `countWords` instead of importing from `src/lib/content.ts` — the two can drift silently.
- **Untested branches:** contiguous-numbering check with gaps/duplicates in `map.chapters`; unknown `categoryId`; chapter file present with no map entry (warning) vs. map entry with no file (error) — these are easy to invert by accident; malformed `book.json` (invalid JSON) handling; short (<800 words) / long (>3200 words) chapter warnings.

```js
// scripts/validate-content.test.mjs
describe('validate-content helpers', () => {
  it('flags non-contiguous map chapter numbers', () => {})
  it('flags a map chapter with no corresponding chapter file (error)', () => {})
  it('warns (not errors) when a chapter file has no map entry', () => {})
  it('errors on invalid JSON in book.json without crashing the whole run', () => {})
})
```

---

sections.title: Priority if you're going to tackle this incrementally
sections.content:
1. **`content.ts` + `progress.ts` + `prefs.ts`** — pure, no mocking, highest bug-catching value per hour (especially `bookStats`' div-by-zero and `sanitize`'s corrupted-storage recovery).
2. **`tts.ts` pure helpers** (`toChunks`, `htmlToBlocks`) — then the state machine, which is where the suspected empty-chunks bug lives.
3. **`store.ts`** — one-time investment, everything else depends on it.
4. **Page-level integration tests** for `Reader`'s autoplay/Escape logic — most user-visible risk, least covered today.
